LAW AND MOTION, DEFENDANTS CHARLES MCMACKIN AND CARROLL HENRY'S DEMURRER TO ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY BONNICI IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION. THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ABOUT THE ALLEGED DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS ARE CONCLUSORY. THE ALLEGATION THAT THE DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS REFER TO MR. BONNICI'S "CONDUCT AS A BUYER AND SELLER OF JEWELRY AND OTHER ITEMS OF VALUE, WITH THE INTENT OF LEADING OTHER SELLERS TO BELIEVE FALSE THAT" MR. BONNICI AND HIS ASSOCIATES "CONDUCT THEIR BUSINESS IN DECEPTIVE, ILLEGAL FALSE AND FRAUDULENT MANNER" PROVIDES VERY LITTLE INFORMATION ABOUT THE CONTENT OF THE ALLEGED DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS AND NONE ABOUT THE PERSONS TO WHOM SUCH STATEMENTS WERE MADE. WHILE MR. BONNICI IS NOT REQUIRED TO PROVIDE EVIDENTIARY FACTS, HE DOES NEED TO PROVIDE SUFFICIENT INFORMATION FOR THE COURT AND DEFENDANTS TO KNOW THE GIST OF THE ALLEGED DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS AND TO WHOM THOSE STATEMENTS WERE MADE. FOR EXAMPLE, DO THE ALLEGED DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS STATE THAT MR. BONNICI LIES TO HIS PROSPECTIVE CUSTOMERS AND/OR SELLS GOODS THAT ARE NOT WHAT HE REPRESENTS THEM TO BE AND/OR BUYS GOODS WITHOUT THE INTENTION OF PAYING FOR THEM? ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION, PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY BONNICI'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE JANUARY 24, 2018 JUDGMENT IS SET ASIDE BASED ON THE MANDATORY RELIEF PROVISIONS OF CCP SECTION 473(B). DEFENDANTS CHARLES MCMACKIN AND CARROLL HENRY ARE ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF FEES AND COSTS OF $5,000 TO BE PAID BY MR. BONNICI'S COUNSEL GINO MOLINARI NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 31, 2018. WHILE THIS IS LESS THAN THE AMOUNT CLAIMED BY DEFENDANTS, THE COURT'S OWN REVIEW OF THE ADDITIONAL WORK CAUSED BY MR. MOLINARI'S ADMITTED FAULT SHOWS THAT THE HIGH RANGE OF REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS FOR THAT WORK IS $5000. ORDER SIGNED. Defendants' Demurrer To Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-25-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re Defendants' Demurrer To Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint.

No appearances made by the parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

Defendants McMackin and Henry's demurrers to plaintiff's third amended complaint are overruled.

The court liberally reads the amended complaint as a whole. (See Code Civ. Proc. 452.) "Defamation is the intentional publication of a statement of fact that is false, unprivileged, and has a natural tendency to injure or that causes special damage." (Grenier v. Taylor (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 471, 486.) Paragraphs 16-17 of the amended complaint plead timely defamation causes of action. Plaintiff is correct that when pleading a defamation claim, less particularity is required where the defendant presumptively possess full information concerning the facts of the controversy. (See Bradley v. Hartford Acc. & Indemnity Co. (1973) 30 Cal.App.3d 818, 825 (disapproved on other grounds in Silberg v. Anderson (1990) 50 Cal.3d 205.) Here, paragraphs 9-15 and 18 allege that defendants have a history of falsely accusing plaintiff of theft. Paragraph 16 alleges that defendant published a statement falsely accusing plaintiff of a crime (theft) to Jude Sykes in July of 2016. Plaintiff pleads defamation per se. (See Civ. Code 46(1).) Paragraph 17 similarly alleges that defendant falsely told a San Mateo homeowner in July of 2016 that defendant was a thief. These instances of defamation are not time-barred and discovery will reveal the specifics of the alleged communications.

The demurrer to the intentional interference with prospective economic advantage cause of action is overruled. To plead the claim, plaintiff must allege: "(1) an economic relationship between plaintiff and a third party, with the probability of future economic benefit to the plaintiff; (2) defendant's knowledge of the relationship; (3) an intentional act by the defendant, designed to disrupt the relationship; (4) actual disruption of the relationship; and (5) economic harm to the plaintiff proximately caused by the defendant's wrongful act, including an intentional act by the defendant that is designed to disrupt the relationship between the plaintiff and a third party." (Edwards v. Arthur Anderson LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, 944.) While not a model of pleading, the amended complaint sufficiently alleges facts showing that defendant intentionally interfered with the prospective economic relationship between plaintiff, on the one hand, and Jude Seyks and the San Mateo homeowner, on the other hand.

Single order signed as to both demurrer and motion to strike.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Charles McMackin and Carroll Henry's demurrer to all four causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Timothy Bonnici is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all four causes of action. The allegations in the complaint about the alleged defamatory statements are conclusory. The allegation that the defamatory statements refer to Mr. Bonnici's "conduct as a buyer and seller of jewelry and other items of value, with the intent of leading other sellers to believe false that" Mr. Bonnici and his associates "conduct their business in deceptive, illegal false and fraudulent manner" provides very little information about the content of the alleged defamatory statements and none about the persons to whom such statements were made. While Mr. Bonnici is not required to provide evidentiary facts, he does need to provide sufficient information for the court and defendants to know the gist of the alleged defamatory statements and to whom those statements were made. For example, do the alleged defamatory statements state that Mr. Bonnici lies to his prospective customers and/or sells goods that are not what he represents them to be and/or buys goods without the intention of paying for them? Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion For Relief From Judgment [Ccp Sec 473(B)]
Off Calendar Sep-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY BONNICI'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM JUDGMENT CCP SEC. 473(b).

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Timothy Bonnici's motion for relief from judgment is granted. The January 24, 2018 judgment is set aside and the case is restored to the civil active list. Mr. Bonnici is entitled to have the judgment set aside based on the mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Mr. Molinari's second declaration of fault adequately establishes that the January 24 judgment was entered against Mr. Bonnici due to the mistake and neglect of Mr. Molinari. In keeping with the remedial purposes of CCP 473(b), the court determines that, while this motion surely could have been filed earlier, relief should not be denied to Mr. Bonnici on the grounds that it was not filed within a reasonable time since the timing of the filing of the motion appears to have been determined by Mr. Molinari, not Mr. Bonnici. Although there is sometimes a fine line between "deliberate tactical choice" and attorney mistake and neglect, Mr. Molinari's errors are more accurately characterized as mistake and neglect than "deliberate tactical choice" and that characterization comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits especially where, as here, there is no assertion of legal prejudice by the non-moving party, nor does it appear that such an assertion could be credibly made. Defendants Carroll Henry and Charles McMackin are entitled to an award of fees and costs per CCP 473(b). There will be a further hearing on October 3, 2018 to determine the amount of that award. Defendants are required to submit a short memorandum and evidence stating the amount they are seeking no later than September 14, 2018. Mr. Bonnici must file any opposition papers no later than September 21, 2018. Defendants must file any reply papers by September 28, 2018. Defendants' demurrer to the complaint will also be re-calendared for October 3, 2018. Mr. Bonnici must file their opposition papers to the demurrer by September 21, 2018 and defendants must file any reply papers by September 28, 2018. Counsel for Mr. Bonnici is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and should be in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
